originalTitle: Three years of messages at once - a chronicle of Sudan's war pours in as trapped reporter's phone turns on
title: A Special Phone Story from Sudan
content:
Hello, Friends!

Hi everyone! I'm your friendly news reporter for Africa! Today, I have a very special story from a country called Sudan. Sudan is a big and beautiful country in Africa, just like many of our homes. It's a place with kind people, amazing history, and lovely animals. But sometimes, sad things happen even in beautiful places. We want to tell you about one brave person there.

Meet Mohamed, Our Special Reporter

Imagine a grown-up who helps tell stories to everyone. That's what a journalist does! Our story is about a brave journalist named Mohamed Suleiman. Mohamed lives in Sudan. His job is to find out what is happening and tell people, so everyone knows. This is a very important job, because it helps us learn and understand.

When Things Get Tough

For a very long time, things have been tough in parts of Sudan. Sometimes, when grown-ups can't agree, they start a "conflict" or a "war." This means it's not safe, and people have to be very careful. During this time, it's hard to talk to people, even your family and friends. Imagine if you couldn't call your grandma or send a message to your best friend for a very, very long time. That's what happened to many people in Sudan, including Mohamed.

No Power, No Calls

For many days, weeks, and even years, it was hard to use phones in Sudan. Sometimes there was no electricity to charge phones. Sometimes the internet didn't work. So, Mohamed's phone was quiet. It was like a sleeping phone, not making any sounds or showing any new messages. He couldn't hear from his friends, his family, or anyone about what was happening far away. He didn't know if they were okay. This must have made him feel very lonely and worried. **Did you know?** Sudan is the third-largest country in Africa by land area! It's super big, so talking to everyone can be tricky even without problems.

The Phone Wakes Up!

Then, something amazing happened! After a very, very long time, Mohamed's phone finally had power. It found a signal, like a little bird finding its way home. *Bzzzzzz!* The phone started to light up. Imagine all the lights turning on at once! It was like a dam breaking open, and instead of water, messages came pouring out!

Messages, Messages Everywhere!

Mohamed's phone started showing messages from three whole years! Three years is a very, very long time. Think about when you were in Class 1, or even younger. All those years, messages were waiting for Mohamed. They came rushing in like a big river. Old messages, new messages, all talking about what happened during the sad time. It was a lot to take in all at once.

What Did the Messages Say?

Some messages were from friends, asking if he was okay. Some were from family, sharing news. But many messages were sad. They told stories about what had been "lost." When we say "lost" here, we don't mean losing a toy. It means much bigger things. Friends had moved away. Houses were broken. Schools couldn't open. Playgrounds were empty. Things that make life happy for children and grown-ups were not there anymore. **Did you know?** The River Nile, one of the most famous rivers in the world, flows through Sudan! It helps make the land green and helps people travel. It's a very important part of the country.

Sadness for What Was Lost

Mohamed felt a big shiver. A shiver is like when you feel a little cold, but this shiver was because of sadness. He read about places he knew that were now changed. He read about people he loved who had suffered. It was like reading a very long, very sad storybook, but it was all real. He felt sad for all the happy times that were replaced by tough times. He worried about his people and his home.

Hope for Sudan

Even though Mohamed felt sad, he is also a brave reporter. He knows that people in Sudan are strong. They love their country and they want peace. Peace means everyone can live happily and safely, go to school, play outside, and visit their friends and family without any worries. It means no more quiet phones because of troubles. Everyone wants to live a happy, peaceful life. **Did you know?** Sudan has more ancient pyramids than Egypt! Imagine so many old, pointy buildings, telling stories of long, long ago. How cool is that? These amazing places are part of Sudan's rich history.

A Wish for Our Friends in Sudan

We want to send our love to Mohamed and all the children and grown-ups in Sudan. We hope that soon, their phones will ring with happy messages. Messages about schools opening again, about new games being played, and about everyone living together in peace. It's important for everyone to have a safe home and a happy life. We wish for a bright and peaceful future for all of them.
summary: Mohamed, a brave reporter in Sudan, saw three years of messages on his phone when it finally turned on, making him sad about the past but hopeful for peace.
whyMatters: This story helps us understand how hard it is for people when there is war, and why it is very important for everyone in Africa and the world to have peace and be safe.

originalTitle: The human cost of the war in Sudan, three years on
title: A Big Fight in Sudan: Helping Our Friends
content:
Hello, Friends!

Hi everyone! Your friendly African news reporter is here. Today, we need to talk about something important that is happening in a country called Sudan. Sudan is a big country in Africa, just like ours! Sometimes, big problems happen in the world, and it's good to learn about them so we can understand and care.

What is Happening in Sudan?

Imagine two groups of people having a very, very big argument. So big that they are fighting. This is what we call a "conflict" or a "war." It started not too long ago, in a year we call 2023. That's just last year! So, it has been going on for a long time now, and it's still making people sad. When grown-ups fight like this, many sad things happen. It's not like a small schoolyard fight. This is a very serious fight between big groups. People get very scared. They can get hurt. They lose their homes. It makes everyone very, very sad.

So Many People Affected

The news reporter, Barbara Plett Usher, from the BBC, said something important. She said that the number of sad things that have happened to people is "staggering." What does "staggering" mean? It means "so, so, so many" sad things. It's a huge number, like counting all the stars in the sky, almost! So many people are hurting. Many, many people in Sudan have had to leave their homes. Imagine having to leave your house, your toys, your bed, and everything you know. They have to go to a safer place, maybe with their families. This is called being "displaced." They don't have a home to go back to right now, which is very tough.

Homes and Food

Think about your home. It's where you feel safe and warm, right? Where you eat yummy food and play with your brothers and sisters. In Sudan, many homes have been damaged or destroyed because of the fighting. This means families don't have a safe place to sleep. And food! It's hard to get food when there's a big fight happening. Shops might be closed. Farmers might not be able to grow their crops safely. So, many children and their families in Sudan are hungry. They don't always know where their next meal will come from. This is a very sad thing to think about.

Did You Know?

Did you know that Sudan is the third-largest country in Africa by land size? It's a very big place! It has beautiful rivers like the River Nile and interesting animals. It's a land full of friendly people who just want to live in peace.

Children Need to Play and Learn

What do children love to do? Play! And learn at school! But when there's a big fight, it's very hard for children in Sudan to do these things. Schools might be closed or not safe to go to. Imagine not being able to go to school and see your friends, or learn new things. Playing outside can be dangerous. Children might not have their toys anymore. They just want to feel safe, eat good food, and play with their friends, just like you. But the conflict makes it very difficult for them to have a normal childhood. They need a lot of comfort and help to feel happy again.

Doctors and Helpers

Even when things are very sad, there are always kind people who try to help. Doctors and nurses work very hard to help people who are hurt. They are super brave! They help bandage wounds and give medicine so people can feel better. Other people, called aid workers, try to bring food, water, and blankets to families who have lost everything. They help build safe shelters too. These helpers come from all over the world, and from Africa too! They want to make sure children and grown-ups in Sudan get the care they need. They are like superheroes, trying to make things a little bit better in a very hard situation.

Did You Know?

Did you know that many brave people work for organisations like UNICEF and the Red Cross? These groups help children and families all around the world when they are in trouble. They bring medicine, food, and clean water, and even set up safe spaces for children to play and learn.

Why is This Happening?

It's hard for grown-ups to understand fully why big fights like this happen. But usually, it's because different groups of people disagree about who should be in charge or how things should be run in their country. Instead of talking and finding a solution, they started fighting. It's always, always better to talk and share, not fight.

What Can We Hope For?

Everyone, especially the children of Sudan, wants the fighting to stop. They want peace. Peace means everyone is safe, happy, and can go back to their homes, schools, and play. It means families can be together without fear, and farmers can grow food again. We can all wish for peace for Sudan. We can think about the children there and hope that they will soon have safe homes, plenty of food, and happy days filled with play and learning. We want them to feel safe and loved, just like we do. It's important to send them our good wishes.

A Message of Love

So, even though the news from Sudan is sad, remember that there are many people who care and are trying to help. We send all our love and wishes for peace to the children and families in Sudan. We hope that very soon, the big fight will end, and they can all live happily ever after in their beautiful country. Let's keep them in our thoughts!
summary: A big fight in Sudan, a country in Africa, has made many children and families sad, losing their homes and food, but kind people are trying to help them and everyone wishes for peace.
whyMatters: It matters because children and families in Sudan are scared and need help, and we should care about our neighbours in Africa and wish for everyone to be safe and happy.

title: South Africa names apartheid-era politician as new ambassador to the US
content: Roelf Meyer played a key role as a negotiator during talks to end white-minority rule in South Africa.

title: Italy suspends long-standing defence agreement with Israel
content: Italy suspended the renewal of a 20-year-long defence agreement with Israel, following recent tensions between them.

originalTitle: What you need to know about Israel’s ambassador to the US
title: Super Friends Talk: Ambassadors and Countries!
content:
Hello, Super Explorers from Africa!

Hey there, awesome adventurers! It's your friend, the news reporter, right here from Africa! Are you ready for some super cool news from around the world? We're going to learn about important people who help countries be friends. Isn't that amazing? Let's zoom into our story!

What is an Ambassador?

Imagine you have a really good friend who lives far away in another town. Your mom or dad might send a special letter or call their mom or dad, right? An ambassador is like that super special messenger! They are very important people. They live in a different country far from their home country. They help their home country talk to the new country. Their job is to make sure everyone understands each other. Ambassadors try to solve problems and share happy news. They are like a bridge between two countries! It's a very big and important job. Did you know? Ambassadors sometimes wear very fancy clothes for important meetings! It's like wearing your best clothes for a special party.

Countries Are Like Big Neighborhoods!

So, what are countries? Think of countries like really, really big neighborhoods on our Earth. We live in Africa! Our big neighborhood is called Africa. There's a country called Israel. And right next door, there's another country called Lebanon. They are neighbors! Then, there's a big country far away called the United States, or the US. It's like different houses on a very, very long street. Did you know? Countries have their own special flags, just like you might have a favorite color! Each flag tells a story about that country.

Meeting Up for a Chat!

Okay, so Israel has a special ambassador who lives in the United States. His name is Yechiel Leiter. He is a very busy helper! Recently, he had a special 'talk' or meeting. Guess who he talked to? He talked with someone from Lebanon! This person from Lebanon is called his 'counterpart'. That just means they do the same kind of job. One works for Israel, and the other works for Lebanon. They sat down to chat, just like you might chat with a friend at school. Talking helps countries work together. They share ideas and try to find ways to help each other. It’s like when you share your toys with a friend so you can both play!

Sometimes Talking Can Be Tricky

Even when we talk with our friends, it can be a little tricky sometimes. Maybe you want to play with your building blocks, but your friend really wants to play with cars. It's hard to agree sometimes, right? Well, some people are saying that Yechiel Leiter, the ambassador from Israel, sometimes finds it a bit tricky to agree with everyone. They think he might not always listen carefully enough to other people's ideas. Or maybe he has very strong ideas of his own. These people are called 'critics'. They just want everyone to talk and listen nicely. But guess what? Even when it's tricky, it's super important to keep trying to understand each other! Just like you and your friend try to find a game you both like.

Why is Talking Super Important?

Why do these important people keep talking, even if it's tricky? Because talking is super, super important! It helps us learn new things about each other. It helps us find ways to share and be kind. When countries talk, they are trying to make the world a safer and happier place for everyone. They want peace. Peace means everyone is safe and happy. It means no one is fighting or being unkind. Did you know? When people from different countries talk, they sometimes use a special person called a translator! A translator helps them understand each other's languages so everyone can talk easily.

Working for Peace Together

It's like when you have a small problem with a friend. You don't just stop being friends, right? You try to talk about it. You try to find a solution so you can both be happy. Maybe you can play with blocks *and* cars! Countries are trying to do the same thing. They want to be good neighbors and friends. They want all the children and grown-ups in Israel, Lebanon, and everywhere else, to live happily and safely. It takes lots of patience and lots of listening. Everyone needs to try their very best to understand. Just like your teachers tell you, listening is key!

Let's Be Kind and Listen!

So, even when it's hard, people like ambassadors keep talking. They are working hard for peace for everyone. They want all the people in Israel and Lebanon to live happily and be good neighbors. Let's all remember to talk nicely, listen carefully, and be kind to everyone. Even when we don't agree right away, we can always try to understand. You can be a super ambassador for kindness in your own neighborhood too! See you next time, future peace-makers!
